evidence.anxiety: cognitive_model.core_belief: 'If I express my full emotional range in this marriage, I will repeat the conflicts that ended the first one.'
evidence.depression: The persona has multiple engaging hobbies and describes his current marriage as 'the most emotionally honest partnership he has had,' indicating no anhedonia or hopelessness.
evidence.trauma: The failure of his first marriage is a specific past event that directly shapes his current coping strategy of withholding emotions to avoid repeating past conflicts.
evidence.attachment_disorganisation: The unmet need is to 'bring his full emotional presence into this marriage without fearing it will end the same way,' which captures a core approach-avoidance conflict.
evidence.escalation_tendency: His sample utterance, 'I want to tell you what I heard, to make sure I got it right before I go further,' is actively de-escalatory and collaborative.

evidence.anxiety: Sometimes fears Helen is settling for him, and that he is a thoughtful, competent consolation after a marriage whose depth he cannot match.
evidence.escalation_tendency: I did not want to escalate. I also did not want to disappear. I am not sure I managed either.
